108.status: Sustained the injury running to first base on June 17 and underwent an MRI exam that revealed a strain. Progressed to light on-field exercises on June 23.
Threw a short bullpen session on June 20 and threw off a mound again on June 23.
Has been hitting on the field, taking grounders and running with no issues. Will start a throwing program on June 24.
Started his throwing program and has progressed to playing catch with a targeted return date shortly after the All-Star break.

Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself trying to beat out an infield single. Progressed to not wearing the boot and playing catch on the field June 10. Has continued light on-field activities.

Said on June 7 his elbow had been bothering him his last three starts when throwing changeups. MRI exam on June 8 revealed a torn ulnar collateral ligament. Underwent successful season-ending Tommy John surgery.
Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona and threw his second bullpen session on June 16.
Underwent CT scan on June 10 that revealed the fracture and will undergo surgery that will keep him out roughly four weeks.
Participated in light on-field activity in early June but ultimately decided to undergo right knee surgery. Can't return until July 21 at the earliest.
Sustained injury playing with Double-A Rocket City on May 31. Added to 40-man roster because he had an opt-out in his Minor League contract and placed on IL.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Felt tightness during June 19 game in Los Angeles, then again on June 20, when he was removed in eighth inning. Out of lineup from June 21-23, but did pregame work latter two days.
Struck by throw on left ear while running to first base in June 18 game in Seattle, then removed from contest. Traveled with team to Los Angeles and Anaheim. Rode exercise bike on June 23.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Optimistic he'll be ready for start of Spring Training 2027. Nearing a running progression as of June 23.
Began rehab assignment with Double-A Chesapeake on June 18 and went 0-for-8 with four walks in three games there. Moved to Triple-A Norfolk on June 23.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3. Progressing to his first bullpen session on June 23.
Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk on June 21. He allowed one run on two hits and two walks with five strikeouts over 3 2/3 innings in a 66-pitch, 38-strike outing.
Began rehab assignment with Double-A Chesapeake on June 21. He tossed 3 1/3 scoreless innings of one-hit ball with three strikeouts in a 43-pitch, 34-strike outing.
Received facet injection to lower back on June 12. Continuing his rehabilitation and beginning a throwing progression.
Routinely throwing bullpen sessions. Progressed to live batting practice on June 19.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Running in Sarasota as of June 13.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continuing to discuss treatment options. No clear timeline for return.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.

111.status: Mayer fouled a ball off his left foot June 22, causing swelling and soreness. Had June 23 off to feel better.

Stretched out to 3 1/3 innings and 53 pitches in his Minor League rehab start for Triple-A Worcester on June 23, giving up four hits, two runs (one earned) while walking one and striking out one. Next start for WooSox scheduled for June 28.
Was re-evaluated after some light throwing, and manager Chad Tracy cited improving numbers, saying he is “improving but not quite ready to throw yet.”
Doing defensive work and hitting right-handed. Building his left-handed swing back up, currently hitting off the tee and soft toss.
Had been dealing with pain and inflammation in arm for past two weeks, but swelling recently got worse. Will stay with team, then undergo more imaging in Boston at the end of the week.
"Progressing pretty nicely," according to interim manager Chad Tracy. Jogging at good pace, has swung off of a tee. Won't be eligible to be activated until just after the All-Star break.
After a recent bullpen session, Morán received a cortisone injection, which could help lessen the remaining inflammation he has. He will go two to three days without throwing.
Started a throwing program in mid-June, but isn't close to a return.
Continues to have right forearm tightness.
Is in the early stages of a hitting program, but not close to a return.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.

112.status: Steele was slated to throw on June 22 for the first time since his setback in April. He will progress through his throwing program before determining his next steps, but he likely won’t be ready to start games by the end of the season.
Worked four scoreless innings and logged 59 pitches for High-A South Bend on June 20. Tossed 30 pitches in bullpen session on June 22. Slated to return on June 24 for series finale vs. Mets.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 3 prospect, No. 84 overall)
112.status: Logged 2 2/3 innings (49 pitches) for High-A South Bend on June 21. Struck out three, walked two and allowed one run in his second outing since returning to game action.

MRI showed a mild flexor strain. Will be shut down through the upcoming road trip (June 22-28) with goal of beginning a throwing program after that. Put on IL after not recovering well from June 15 outing.
Exited his June 16 outing vs. Rockies due to a cramp in his pitching hand. Was feeling improved on June 17 and hope is Cabrera can go through usual routine before next start.

Exited June 7 start early and imaging showed a "moderate" strain, per manager Craig Counsell. Taillon has continued plyo ball work and will head on next road trip with Cubs with goal of building up his throwing.
Has resumed playing catch after feeling some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

114.status: Has resumed hitting, running and throwing. No target date for return, but team hopeful he can return during 10-game homestand that begins June 26.
Underwent surgery on June 16. Manager Stephen Vogt said the expectation is roughly a six-week recovery.
Additional MRI and CT imaging revealed non-displaced fracture after he exited June 13 game against Detroit. Manager Stephen Vogt said Martínez is expected to miss 4-6 weeks.
Needs right elbow surgery. Set to meet with Dr. Keith Meister on July 2 to determine full scope of procedure and the next steps.

115.status: Scheduled to begin rehab assignment in Triple-A Albuquerque on June 23.
Scheduled to begin rehab assignment in Triple-A Albuquerque on June 23.
Had surgery June 22 to have an internal brace put in his elbow. Expected recovery is one year.More >
Has shown strike-throwing ability in most of his 10 appearances. Earned a win in his last outing on June 16.
Will receive a follow-up MRI at the end of June, with hopes of being cleared for increased activity.
Faced hitters for three innings at Coors Field on June 20, and will begin a Minor League rehab assignment for Triple-A Albuquerque on June 24.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Underwent an MRI June 16 as doctors try to get a handle on what has stopped his rehab from advancing.
Receiving a second opinion on the UCL tear.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 15 overall)
115.status: After receiving the results of multiple examinations, will undergo season-ending surgery, a source told MLB.com. Recovery period will last until late in the year.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
No return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Scratched from scheduled return June 21 after suffering a left hamstring strain during June 17 side session. Playing catch and completing rehab daily.
Allowed two runs on three hits with seven strikeouts over 5 2/3 innings in June 23 rehab start for Double-A Erie.
Completing a return-to-play throwing progression. Transferred to 60-day injured list on June 23.
Started a throwing progression on June 22.
Struck by therapy band below left eye during postgame arm-care routine June 16. Returned to Detroit and is under evaluation. Not expected to suffer long-term damage to eyesight.
Received a cortisone injection on June 19. Missed four weeks with a strain to the same oblique earlier this season.
Completed three days of deloading before resuming throwing on June 16. Will throw a bullpen session on June 20.
Initiated a return to a throwing progression on June 15.
Underwent a hydrodissection procedure on June 10 to address lingering soreness in elbow. Expected to resume playing catch the following week.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9.

117.status: Exited June 22 against the Blue Jays during his at-bat in the sixth inning. Out of starting lineup June 23, but entered in the eighth inning and drew a walk.
Expected to begin rehab games in the Florida Complex League over the weekend after rehabbing at the Astros’ facility in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Expected to begin rehab games over the weekend. Had been taking batting practice and fielding ground balls.
Threw 75 pitches over 4 2/3 innings of three-run ball on June 21, striking out five. The Astros will see how he feels in the coming days before deciding his next steps.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Will make rehab start June 25 at Double-A Corpus Christi after throwing three scoreless innings in his first rehab start June 19 for the Rookie-level FCL Astros.
Will make first Minor League rehab start June 25 for Triple-A Sugar Land.
Taking batting practice and shagging fly balls on the field, and will be sent on a rehab assignment soon.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Threw live BP on June 20 in West Palm Beach, Fla., after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. Will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.
Has started a throwing progression. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Missed three games recently before returning to lineup on June 22. However, he was not recovering as hoped. Will undergo additional testing on June 25 in Kansas City.
Went through on-field drills on June 22 and 23, but sat out his fourth straight game.
Shut down since discomfort symptoms returned June 12. Team anticipates surgical procedure on his left elbow. Too soon to say whether he will be able to return this season.
Sustained setback with shoulder issue after starting rehab assignment with Triple-A Omaha on June 9, but MRI exam was clean. Will resume rehab assignment on June 24.
Threw bullpen session June 16, but then experienced same soreness/pinching feeling that put him on IL. Had injection in his shoulder and began playing catch again on June 22.
Suffered shoulder injury while rehabbing foot injury May 6. Threw a bullpen session on June 21 and will throw another on June 24.

Diagnosed with Grade 3 tear of a portion of left plantar fasciitis, which is a severe tear. Began swinging June 17 but has not progressed to full baseball activity. Will likely be a few more weeks until running begins.

118.injury: Fractured hamate bone in right hand
118.status: Sustained injury during an at-bat June 13 and had surgery to remove broken hamate bone on June 14. Has a 4-6 week return-to-play timeline.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Exited the June 22 game in the second inning and will miss the rest of the series in Minnesota. Dodgers are hopeful he can return June 26 in San Diego if he can resume baseball activities June 24.

Removed before the third inning June 22 after taking a foul tip to the face mask. Undergoing evaluation. Cleared initial testing. Second evaluation scheduled for June 23 and could be available if cleared.
Will not travel with the Dodgers on their three-city road trip. He could be activated during the homestand beginning on July 2.
Set to begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Oklahoma City on June 23 and could be activated the following week.
Imaging revealed no structure damage in his elbow, just inflammation due to wear and tear.
Pitched on back-to-back days from June 18-19 with Single-A Ontario, making it likely that he will be activated on June 22 in Minnesota.
Threw off the mound to a standing catcher on June 20 as he gets closer to throwing his first full bullpen session.
Threw a 15-pitch bullpen session, all fastballs, on June 19 as he continues to ramp up his throwing progression.
Has made little progress in his rehab and is currently not throwing.
Joined Triple-A Oklahoma City to begin a rehab assignment on June 4. Will likely take the maximum 30 days to build up.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, but he has had trouble ramping up.

119.injury: Recovery from right shoulder surgery, lower back surgery
119.status: Underwent a surgical procedure on his back, he shared on social media.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

120.status: Herz has been shut down after an MRI revealed a left flexor strain following his first rehab start for Single-A Fredericksburg. Herz underwent Tommy John surgery in April 2025.
Was back in the Nationals' clubhouse prior to the series opener against the Phillies on June 22, and will throw a bullpen session on June 23.

Threw a bullpen June 21 in West Palm Beach, Fla. Butera was impressed with his progress and velocity, and said his status is being evaluated week to week. Kranick was in the Nationals' clubhouse on June 22.
Continuing throwing progression at Nationals' training complex.

Was not in lineup June 21, but was available for reserve duty and does not expect to miss significant time as it's a pain tolerance issue. Exited June 20 game against Rays after running into left-center-field wall making a catch.
Threw off the mound for the first time last week, on schedule with throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
On schedule with continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL.

121.status: Will return from the IL this weekend against the Phillies, either when first eligible June 27 or the next day. Ramped up with a series of bullpen sessions.

Played rehab games June 19 and 21, had a high-intensity workout June 22 and will return to Syracuse Tuesday for what could be his final rehab game. That would set him up for a midweek return -- as soon as Wednesday if he doesn't need rest, potentially Thursday if he does.

Began playing light catch in June but was "still in the early stages" of his rehab, per manager Carlos Mendoza. Expects to undergo reimaging at the six-week mark on June 26. Could begin his ramp-up after that.
Began a Minor League rehab assignment on June 19 and played again June 21 for Triple-A Syracuse. Will play more games in the Minors the week of June 22.

Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Sent to Single-A St. Lucie for rehab assignment on June 18 and advanced to Triple-A Syracuse on June 21. Will require additional time in the Minors.
Continues to take batting practice as he looks to return in a DH-only capacity, but was not close to restarting a Minor League rehab assignment as of a June 15 update.
Began baseball activities in early June but remains on a fluid timeline depending on the progression of his symptoms. Expects to play again this season.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Was initially given a 4-8-week timeline but was still not throwing as of an early June update.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Still not performing any baseball activities as of June 23. A's hope to have a better idea of where he's at in his recovery during the week of June 29.
Started to play catch and a swinging progression during the week of June 22. Will be reassessed after the All-Star break.
Began a throwing progression on June 22. A's are hopeful he could return when first eligible on July 3.
MRI on June 1 revealed a strain of the shoulder capsule and subscapularis muscle. Received a PRP injection on June 4 and will be re-evaluated 4-6 weeks from that date. Moved to 60-day IL June 21.
Began a throwing progression on June 11. No timeline for return.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Exited after being struck by a line drive against the Rockies on June 21. Initial images read as negative, and follow-up reports in Pittsburgh on June 22 showed the same. Played catch on June 23. Will throw a regularly scheduled bullpen on June 24 ahead of a possible next start on June 27.
Began throwing from 90 feet on June 10, extended to 150 feet with no pain while rehabbing in Florida. Will begin a rehab assignment with Double-A Altoona on June 24.

134.injury: Left hand fourth and fifth metacarpal non-displaced fractures
134.status: Has been receiving treatment from the club’s medical staff. Cleared to begin gripping activities on June 22.

Said to be “trending in the right direction” by manager Don Kelly. Threw a bullpen session on June 20 up to 96 mph, according to general manager Ben Cherington. Threw a two-up, 30-pitch bullpen on June 23. The team hopes to get Dotel into a rehab assignment starting the weekend of June 26, per athletic trainer Todd Tomczyk.

Transferred from 15-day IL to 60-day on June 7 to make room for infielder Davis Wendzel on the 40-man roster. Began side progression the first week of June. Expected to throw live batting practice first week of July.

Saw local orthopedics in Indianapolis. Information was sent to Dr. Keith Meiser, who performed his Tommy John surgery in 2022. Ligament is not an issue, per athletic trainer Todd Tomczyk. Will not throw for five to seven days.

135.status: Experienced elbow soreness after his outing on June 21. Per manager Craig Stammen, "will be down from throwing for a couple days, then get back at it.”

Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Began playing catch in late May and played long toss on June 23.
Has made four rehab appearances with a 1.76 ERA for Triple-A El Paso. Is slated for at least one more.
Sidelined after an MRI revealed a flexor strain. Began playing catch in late May, and played long toss on June 23. Could throw off a mound soon.
Experienced concussion symptoms after taking a fastball off the chin on April 18. Has progressed to live batting practice at the club's Spring Training complex in Arizona.
Began throwing program in late May. Struck out two over three innings in June 19 rehab start with Triple-A El Paso.
Continuing his recovery at the team's complex in Arizona but is not yet scheduled for a rehab assignment.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn labrum in his right hip on June 5. First sustained the injury in 2021, but it recurred and worsened over the season's first two months. Likely to miss the remainder of the season.
Began experiencing knee soreness in late May, and landed on the IL when it worsened. Team is optimistic his absence won't be lengthy.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: On verge of beginning a running progression that is likely to last 10-14 days during Mariners' current homestand, while also doing pregame fielding and hitting drills. Should be ready for a rehab assignment if all goes well.More »

Moved rehab to club's Spring Training facility in Arizona. Will wait three to four weeks before undergoing another MRI. Current injury is a more serious strain in a different part of his lat than his injury earlier this season.More »
Injury "kind of came out of nowhere" ahead of June 9 game, per GM Justin Hollander. Will undergo another MRI in three to four weeks.
Has begun a throwing program. On a similar timeline for return to Matt Brash and Cooper Criswell.
Pulled off rehab assignment due to pain in his finger. Meeting with doctor, and if injury isn't making progress, he'll need season-ending surgery.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Scheduled to throw a bullpen on June 20 and expected to rejoin Giants' rotation on June 24.
Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on June 17. Main priority is getting additional game action, according to Giants manager Tony Vitello.
Reported to Arizona to continue his rehab. Will start a throwing progression by the end of this weekend.
Saw Dr. Robert Anderson on June 5 and received a stem cell injection. Currently rehabbing in Arizona.
Continuing to rehab with Triple-A Sacramento.
Underwent surgery in Arizona on June 4 to remove the fractured hamate bone from his left wrist.
Hasn't played for Triple-A Sacramento since May 23 due to the wrist fracture.
Will miss two to three weeks with the adductor strain.
Sustained the injury fielding a ground ball between the mound and first base on May 31.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Winn was scratched from Tuesday’s lineup with left thumb irritation, per manager Oliver Marmol. At this point, severity is unknown.
Fernandez started a rehab assignment with Triple-A Memphis on June 19 after throwing side sessions on June 15 and 17.
Had a setback during his rehab and is now dealing with a left elbow issue similar to the right elbow injury that sent him to the IL. He was transferred to the 60-day IL on June 12.

139.status: Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11, then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. Had two at-bats against Craig Kimbrel in live BP on June 9 and aiming to start rehab assignment June 29.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, 2025, with target return around midseason. Scheduled to face hitters in live batting practice on June 30.
Scratched from May 16 lineup and placed on IL May 17. Manager Kevin Cash initially said he could require 6-8 weeks of recovery after the operation. Doing well and progressing as tolerated.

Reported soreness in back of right shoulder on April 15. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Had a setback while playing catch from 75 feet, so he underwent a PRP injection. Should resume throwing before the end of June.

Felt soreness during recent series in New York and while pitching in Baltimore on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation. Threw a 20-pitch bullpen on June 8 but not yet nearing a return to game action. Hopes to resume catch play June 20.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Advanced from playing catch on flat ground to a mound progression.

After being designated for assignment on May 29 following an outing on May 27, Heasley was returned to the roster on June 2 due to an injury he suffered while active with the Rays and placed on the 15-day IL with no timeline for his return. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 8.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

140.status: Attempted to pitch through ankle injury before aggravating it when slipping on an on-deck circle making a play. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on June 23 to remove loose body.
Began rehab assignment at Double-A Frisco on June 21, allowing one hit in one scoreless inning.

Took live BP in Miami June 23. Simulated defensive innings by taking ground balls, waiting in the dugout, then returning to the field for more ground ball work. Still needs to check some boxes before he returns from mild concussion symptoms.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but was cleared to begin his progression again in early May. Expected to throw live BP in coming weeks.
Suffered multiple fractures on the pinky finger of his right hand after being hit by a pitch in June 13 game in Boston. Will receive additional X-rays/scans when the team returns home.
Suffered the injury after making a diving catch and landing awkwardly in June 12 game at Fenway Park.
Last appeared in a June 9 loss to the Royals, giving up a go-head homer to Jac Caglianone.
Felt something in his arm after a June 1 start in St. Louis. Scans showed elbow inflammation and he will continue to be monitored over the next 10 days.

Martin missed 30 games (April 15 to May 19) previously. He allowed four earned runs in four appearances (four innings) since returning, surrendering seven hits and two walks without recording a strikeout.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23. Moved to 60-day IL on May 31.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.

141.status: Garcia's long, slow rehab is on pause. He felt soreness after his last rehab appearance and is now meeting with Blue Jays doctors.
Could begin rehab with Single-A Dunedin as early as Thursday.
Threw a bullpen session Monday in Toronto and is "feeling better." Next steps being determined.
Began rehab assignment on Monday and went 0-for-3 as DH in the Complex League.
Will begin hitting over the next week or two in hopes he can be a factor in late 2026.
Made his first game appearance in 706 days with FCL affiliate June 16, throwing one inning. He will continue to build up on schedule now.
Will be having a scope surgical procedure on his knee after receiving a second medical opinion. It is not believed to be season-ending.
Underwent full Tommy John surgery on May 20 and will miss 12 to 18 months.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Threw extended bullpen session June 23 and will throw normal bullpen session June 26. Could begin facing hitters soon.
MRI showed renewed swelling in elbow but no damage to UCL or flexor tendon. Receiving second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister before determining next steps, but arthroscopic surgery a possibility.
Pain-free and could appear in a game as early as June 28.
MRI on June 12 showed healing but also inflammation in the forearm. Will throw bullpen session on June 24.
Underwent followup exam and signs are promising but is still a ways off.
Threw bullpen session June 23 but still faces “a multi-week progression” before facing hitters.
Has thrown four bullpen sessions, could begin facing hitters the week of June 28.
Has begun taking overhand batting practice and caught a bullpen session on June 22. His return date remains uncertain.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.

143.status: Was a late scratch from starting lineup on June 23 with lower back tightness.
Added to the IL before June 16 game against Marlins. Imaging came back clean. President of baseball operations Dave Dombrowski said he anticipates Keller returning as soon as eligible.

Injured on June 10 after attempting to throw out a runner at the plate. Will undergo season-ending surgery on June 24. A free agent at season's end, García is expected to be fully ready for 2027 season.
Experienced right elbow discomfort while ramping up baseball activities for return from 80-game suspension. Surgery with internal brace is required.

143.injury: Discogenic pain and facet inflammation in back
143.status: Underwent procedure on June 5 to address recurring back issue. Phillies hope he returns to Minor League game action in six to eight weeks.

144.status: Didn’t accompany Braves on the current road trip, which runs through June 28. Team is being cautious as he deals with his second hamstring strain since the start of May. Might not return until after the All-Star break.
Will likely be sidelined through the end of July, according to manager Walt Weiss, and may not return until August.
Recorded three strikeouts in a scoreless inning while making his second Minor League rehab appearance for Double-A Columbus on June 23. Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Began a rehab assignment June 23 in the catcher position as he attempts to boost his chance of returning to the roster. Has appeared in 19 career games behind the plate, including 15 in 2019.
Evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister on June 15 and prescribed one month of rest. If his MRI is clean when he is re-evaluated in July, Strider will be cleared to begin throwing progressions.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. Was in New York on June 13 for a doctor's visit when Atlanta played the Mets.
Has started throwing bullpen sessions, has begun to face hitters in live batting practice as well.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. Began throwing in early May and is up to 75 feet in his throwing progression.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.

145.status: Hit head against wall making a leaping catch in June 17 game against the Yankees.
Was struck on the upper arm by a line drive in the June 17 game against the Yankees.
Had started performing baseball activities (running, throwing, hitting off a tee) by June 17, per manager Will Venable.
Pitched 1 2/3 innings of two-run ball for Triple-A Charlotte on June 16 to begin his rehab assignment, allowing one hit, walking three and striking out four.
Hit two-run homer and went 2-for-3 to begin rehab assignment June 16 with Triple-A Charlotte.
Replaced on the roster by fellow southpaw Chris Murphy.
Pulled from his injury rehab assignment and sent back to Chicago for further evaluation on June 1 after continuing to experience lower-body soreness.
Has felt discomfort -- off and on -- for the past five weeks, but it flared up while warming up on May 28. He will not throw for the next four weeks.
Recently underwent an appendectomy, but has resumed throwing. The procedure did delay his rehab from Tommy John surgery, though.
Had a setback in his rehab process likely pushing him into the second half of the season. His timeline is uncertain at this point.
First injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Will be reinstated from the IL to start the series finale against the Rangers. Threw a bullpen session on Monday and touched 95 mph.
Injured during warm-up drills on May 31. Timeline for return to play was approximately six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 21.

Threw pitch design session on June 19 in Jupiter, Fla. Next step would be live batting practice, then a couple of rehab starts. Began throwing off a mound the week of June 15 in light bullpen session.
Estimated timeline for return was 12-16 weeks from date of injury.More >
First felt the discomfort in mid-April in Atlanta but it never affected his pitching, just the build-up. Had trouble breathing leading up to testing. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1.More >

146.injury: Left elbow UCL repair surgery with an internal brace
146.status: Underwent surgery on May 22 as recommended by Dr. Keith Meister. Recovery period calls for 10-12 months.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Left the June 22 game in Detroit after being hit on the wrist by a pitch in the seventh inning. X-rays were negative, and a CT scan is scheduled. Placed on paternity list June 23.

Will join the club for its series in Boston to perform baseball activities such as batting practice, baserunning and shagging fly balls “with an eye toward” ramping it up next week, manager Aaron Boone said.
Tossed bullpen June 19 at Yankee Stadium. Aiming to resume facing hitters by the end of June or early in July. “He feels great,” manager Aaron Boone said. “Every check mark has gone well for him.”

147.playerName: George Lombard Jr. (Yankees' No. 1 prospect, MLB's No. 18)
147.status: Left June 16 game for Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre in fifth inning, one inning after having glove knocked off on a play at second. Sustained two sprained fingers on left hand, according to Yankees spokesperson.
Resumed throwing weekend of June 13-14. Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.
Threw a side session on June 17 and is "a couple of weeks out" from facing hitters, manager Aaron Boone said.
Experienced setback running bases on June 11, manager Aaron Boone said on June 13. Imaging June 15 revealed a re-injury of the calf. Will have a light workload week as a result.

147.injury: Stress fracture of first rib on right side
147.status: After a period of rest and recovery, will be evaluated again in 4-6 weeks (approximately July 3-July 17) to determine level of healing and appropriate next steps.

158.status: Had been optioned to Triple-A Nashville, but that was reversed when he reported discomfort. Scheduled for a bullpen session June 24, then batting practice the weekend of June 27-28, followed by a rehab assignment. (last updated: June 23)
Scheduled to begin a Minor League rehabilitation assignment on June 28 with a multi-inning outing. (last updated: June 23)

Next Minor League outing is scheduled for June 24, though manager Pat Murphy suggested Koenig may rejoin the Brewers instead. Either way, the last day of Koenig's 30-day Minor League rehabilitation assignment is June 27. (last updated: June 23)

Underwent successful but season-ending thoracic outlet decompression surgery on June 22 in Dallas. Based on a typical recovery, Priester should be ready for the start of next season. (last updated: June 23)
First scheduled bullpen session of 15-20 pitches was June 23. (last updated: June 23)
Received anti-inflammatory injection on June 12 in his pectoral minor and had return-to-throw testing scheduled for June 23. (last updated: June 23)

Returned from the IL on May 31 only to feel discomfort during a pregame throwing session and land back on the IL. Was scheduled for a second bullpen session (25 pitches) on June 23. (last updated: June 23)

Recent imaging revealed the development of torn cartilage at his kneecap. Scheduled for arthroscopic surgery on June 25 that pushes his expected return from late June to late July. (last updated: June 23)

Exited June 5 appearance warming up for the seventh inning and diagnosed with a partial proximal UCL sprain. Will get a second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister on June 30 to determine whether Tommy John surgery is needed. (last updated: June 16)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 31)
